issue_title: S-Expression parser can be very slow
issue_body:
The Lisp S-Expression parser (`delphin.util.SExpr`) is very simply defined using the Pegre library (included at `delphin/lib/`) and it is used to parse the output of ACE. It generally works well and is reasonably fast, but it becomes very slow when ACE outputs a lot of results for an item. In the Jacy `mrs` testsuite, item `811` has 1195 results with a recent version of Jacy (most items have ~1--8 results, and the second highest number is 56 results). Parsing this profile using PyDelphin for interfacing ACE takes about 2 minutes, most of which is spent on the one pathological item. In contrast, using ACE with `art` takes less than 2 **seconds**.

This drastic difference warrants replacing the Pegre-based parser with something else. The only other part of PyDelphin using Pegre is the EDS codec. If both of these use something else, the Pegre dependency can be removed.

issue_title: conda update --all does not ignore anaconda version
issue_body:
In my previous understanding and practice, the `conda update anaconda` will update the anaconda to the newest version, and if I installed some new version of packages, they will be downgraded to the version that matches the anaconda version. On the other hand, `conda update --all` will ignore anaconda version, install the newest version of all other packages, and turn anaconda version into `custom`. That was how I updated packages previously. 
However, when I update anaconda to `5.0`, the `conda update --all` works the same as the `conda update anaconda`, it will turn my `custom` anaconda into `5.0`, and downgrade all my other packages. This happens both on my windows and osx laptop. I wonder whether it is a bug, or a new feature?

issue_title: Conda install --clone doesn't respect dependency order

issue_title: `conda install conda` raises UnsatisfiableError
issue_body:
While I was looking into whether our CI setup was compatible with the latest conda release, I run into a rather weird problem that on python 3.6 ``conda install conda`` with the version pinned to ``4.3.23`` in the pinned file raised:

UnsatisfiableError: The following specifications were found to be in conflict

https://github.com/astropy/ci-helpers/blob/master/travis/setup_dependencies_common.sh#L49

issue_title: Error using conda env create -n NAME
issue_body:
Same than https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/3859 but I reopen it because it seems to me that the issue is not solved.
I understand the possibility to use ``conda create -n NAME`` but all conda documentation indicates to use ``conda env create -n NAME`` which lead, in my case, to the error :

```
Using Anaconda API: https://api.anaconda.org

SpecNotFound: Invalid name, try the format: user/package
```

issue_title: document per URL proxy config
issue_body: Conda proxy configuration per URL needs to be documented, as discussed in https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/1527 by @bkreider @jasongrout and others. Also created issue https://github.com/conda/conda-docs/issues/231 in the conda-docs repo to track it. To start we need someone who understands the functionality to write a brief plain text first draft of what should be added to the docs. @jasongrout , can you please jot down a first draft? Posting it as a reply here is fine.

issue_title: Why does fetching repodata take so long?
issue_body:
@stefanseefeld asked

> Working on #5104 I noticed that a repeated call to conda install ... would each time spend considerable time fetching the repodata. How is this done exactly ? Does it download the full data each time ? Wouldn't it be useful to first download a checksum, or somehow timestamp the repodata to avoid redundant downloads ?

issue_title: Navigator: The following conda create command should work?
issue_body:
**On conda 4.0.2**

On navigator we explicitly pass the channels to conda commands so when creating a new env with both python and R, the command that runs is

```bash
conda create --yes --quiet --json --mkdir --name rpy35 python=3.5 jupyter r-essentials --override-channels --channel https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro --channel https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free --channel https://conda.anaconda.org/r
```

This results in **Using Anaconda Cloud api site https://api.anaconda.org** and it just hangs...

I don't fully understand why that command hangs while this one, (assuming no condarc exists)

```bash
conda create --name rpy35 python=3.5 jupyter r-essentials --channel https://conda.anaconda.org/r
```

Works...

They should be in theory the exact same thing under the hood, I am missing something @kalefranz?

issue_title: importing `conda.cli.python_api` changes root logger
issue_body:
`conda.gateways.logging.initialize_logging` calls `initialize_root_logger` and `initialize_conda_logger` which add custom handlers named "stderr" to the root and "conda" logger and change their levels.
`initialize_logging` gets called when importing `conda.gateways`, `conda.gateways.logging`  and `conda.cli.python_api`.

This is problematic when `conda.cli.python_api.run_command` is incorporated in a program which also uses `logging` (e.g., defining custom formatters, etc.). See:
```python
# uses separate processes to re-init `logging` via `basicConfig`
from multiprocessing import Process


def run(level):
    from logging import basicConfig, getLogger
    awesome_format = "\n>>> AWESOME %(levelname)s: %(message)s"
    basicConfig(format=awesome_format, level=level)
    logger = getLogger(__file__)
    msg = "some message"

    def log():
        logger.debug(msg)
        logger.info(msg)
        logger.warn(msg)
        logger.error(msg)

    log()
    import conda.cli.python_api
    #import conda.gateways
    #import conda.gateways.logging
    log()


for level in ("INFO", "WARN", "ERROR"):
    print("{:-^60}".format(level))
    process = Process(target=run, args=(level,))
    process.start()
    process.join()
    print("\n\n")
```
Output (notice the duplicate `ERROR` messages (with `conda`'s logging format) and omitted `WARNING`s):
```
----------------------------INFO----------------------------

>>> AWESOME INFO: some message

>>> AWESOME WARNING: some message

>>> AWESOME ERROR: some message

>>> AWESOME INFO: No cio_test package found.

>>> AWESOME ERROR: some message
ERROR cli_python_api_logging.py:log(16): some message



----------------------------WARN----------------------------

>>> AWESOME WARNING: some message

>>> AWESOME ERROR: some message

>>> AWESOME ERROR: some message
ERROR cli_python_api_logging.py:log(16): some message



---------------------------ERROR----------------------------

>>> AWESOME ERROR: some message

>>> AWESOME ERROR: some message
ERROR cli_python_api_logging.py:log(16): some message



```

issue_title: Document that $CONDA_BLD_PATH must end with "/conda-bld" (and stop breaking stuff with minor releases)
issue_body:
On September 30, @kalefranz inserted a new assertion (`name == 'conda-bld'`) into `context.py` ([see here](https://github.com/conda/conda/blame/master/conda/base/context.py#L299)) causing `conda info` to fail when `CONDA_BLD_PATH` does not end with `/conda-bld`:
```console
$ CONDA_BLD_PATH=/tmp conda info
An unexpected error has occurred.
Please consider posting the following information to the
conda GitHub issue tracker at:

    https://github.com/conda/conda/issues



`$ /home/user/conda/bin/conda info`




    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/exceptions.py", line 479, in conda_exception_handler
        return_value = func(*args, **kwargs)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 145, in _main
        exit_code = args.func(args, p)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main_info.py", line 225, in execute
        channels = list(prioritize_channels(channels).keys())
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 380, in prioritize_channels
        channel = Channel(chn)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 161, in __call__
        c = Channel.from_value(value)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 211, in from_value
        return Channel.from_url(value)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 196, in from_url
        return parse_conda_channel_url(url)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 132, in parse_conda_channel_url
        configured_token) = _read_channel_configuration(scheme, host, port, path)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 97, in _read_channel_configuration
        for name, channel in sorted(context.custom_channels.items(), reverse=True,
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/_vendor/auxlib/decorators.py", line 265, in new_fget
        cache[inner_attname] = func(self)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/base/context.py", line 265, in custom_channels
        all_sources = self.default_channels, (self.local_build_root_channel,), custom_channels
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/_vendor/auxlib/decorators.py", line 265, in new_fget
        cache[inner_attname] = func(self)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/base/context.py", line 246, in local_build_root_channel
        assert name == 'conda-bld'
    AssertionError

```
This change was not documented and led to a hard to trace failure of our test suite (we set `$CONDA_BLD_PATH` in our test suite and `conda info` is, e.g., run by `conda build`). 

Unfortunately, this was not the first time that conda/conda-build introduced subtle, but breaking changes in a patch release.

issue_title: conda on Windows should use the windows network stack for proxy settings
issue_body:
In windows, every application that connects over the internet goes through the Windows networking stack.  The proxy server settings are set in a central location in windows under 

Start → Control Panel → Network and Internet → Internet Options → Connections → LAN Settings

conda should use the same central location to use the proxy server and not require users to manually add proxy information to .condarc

issue_title: activate: not an identifier bug
issue_body:
Bug with zsh (Version 5.0.2 on CentOS 7) and oh-my-zsh, when activating an environment:

`miniconda3/bin/activate:65: not an identifier: %{$fg[cyan]%}%n%{$reset_color%}:%{$fg[green]%}%c%{$reset_color%}$(git_prompt_info)`

Changing 
`export CONDA_PS1_BACKUP=$PS1`
to
`export CONDA_PS1_BACKUP="$PS1"`

Fixed my issue, but I didn't manage to make a pull request.

issue_title: ctable dtype is broken for tables with multidimensional columns
issue_body:
Apologies I don't have time to submit a PR with tests right now, but I believe the dtype property on the ctable class does not correctly handle the case where a table column has more than one dimension. This causes an attempt to load data from a ctable into a numpy array via `__getitem__` to fail.

To work around this I am currently monkey-patching the ctable.dtype property with the following code:

```python
def _ctable_dtype(self):
    """The data type of this object (numpy dtype)."""
    names, cols = self.names, self.cols
    l = []
    for name in names:
        col = cols[name]
        if col.ndim == 1:
            t = (name, col.dtype)
        else:
            t = (name, (col.dtype, col.shape[1:]))
        l.append(t)
    return np.dtype(l)

bcolz.ctable.dtype = property(_ctable_dtype)
```

issue_title: Non-standard, uppercase RG header record tags stripped silently.
FastaFile filepath_index parameter bug
issue_body:
I'm having some trouble using the filepath_index argument of FastaFile: `pysam.FastaFile(filename, filepath_index=index)`. It appears that pysam checks for the presence of the file, but then generates a `.fai` and a `.gzi` for the Fasta alongside it regardless.

If you touch a file named like an index next to the Fasta and `chmod` it so it can't be rewritten an IOError will occur.

I wrote up a demonstration showing that the filepath_index argument might not be handled properly:

issue_title: Adding an option to set index.merge.scheduler.max_thread_count
issue_body:
A common case for Curator is to move indices from Hot nodes to Warm nodes. A common scenario is that the Hot nodes are backed by SSD disks and the Warm nodes are backed by spinning disks. For spinning disks, it is recommended to have the option `index.merge.scheduler.max_thread_count` set to 1.
This parameter in version 5 is an index level parameter, only dynamically settable - when trying to set it in the yml files, the following error happens: 
```
Since elasticsearch 5.x index level settings can NOT be set on the nodes
configuration like the elasticsearch.yaml, in system properties or command line
arguments.In order to upgrade all indices the settings must be updated via the
/${index}/_settings API. Unless all settings are dynamic all indices must be closed
in order to apply the upgradeIndices created in the future should use index templates
to set default values. 
```

It would be great to be able to configure this parameter, so in the Hot(ssd)-Warm(spinning) architecture it can be tuned to have the optimal value in the warm nodes.

issue_title: Urllib3HttpConnection maxsize is not the actual max size
issue_body:
Per the urllib3 docs:

> **maxsize** – Number of connections to save that can be reused. More than 1 is useful in multithreaded situations. If block is set to False, more connections will be created but they will not be saved once they’ve been used.

This means that passing `maxsize` will not actually limit your pool to `maxsize` connections per host. That's just how many it keeps alive. If your pool is being exhausted, it will indefinitely spawn new connections and throw them away, as the default value of `block` is `False`. `Urllib3HttpConnection` currently does not expose the parameter directly, or the ability to indirectly pass it through `**kwargs` so there is no way to configure it.

issue_title: SSL client auth problem in version 5.5.1
issue_body:
With version `5.5.1` it is not possible to use SSL client auth (`client_cert` and `client_key`) when connection with `verify_certs=False`.

As [we can see in the code](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/5.5.1/elasticsearch/connection/http_urllib3.py#L76) if  we set  `verify_certs=False` then ssl client auth will be ignored. 

Also this behaviuor is undocumented, see [`5.5.1` docs](http://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.io/en/5.5.1/connection.html#urllib3httpconnection-default-connection-class).

But in version `6.0.0` this configuration is possible ([see code](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/6.0.0/elasticsearch/connection/http_urllib3.py#L107) ).

The changes was introduced in `6.0.0` with [this commit](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/commit/7339a272b58536921cc6a4f50d0aa12c67483566#diff-e03df718a08bdad9d78d94c50dd7db90).

Nevertheless the behaviour introduced in `6.0.0` seems correct, or at least removes an undocumented feature and permit a more flexible usage of the library, and should be backported also `5.x` releases.

issue_title: Make csv_source_to_csv() skip rows with the wrong number of columns
issue_body:
The Python CSV reader currently barfs on some Korean source files. Each row should only have 12 columns but some look like they have 13, probably because they are not properly quoted. The Python code should just skip those rows rather than throwing an exception and aborting the whole file. It'd be nice to do this in a way that prints warnings or something, so that the code isn't hiding the fact that it can't fully process the input data.

See [https://github.com/openaddresses/openaddresses/issues/851](Openaddresses Issue #851) for exhaustive detail on the Korean sources.

issue_title: Serve compressed CSV
issue_body:
Product proposal: we should serve people compressed output files. Unfortunately S3 won't do this for us, in particular it won't do gzip compression in HTTP. There's a bunch of workarounds, I think the best choice for us is to stop serving `out.csv` files and instead serve `out.csv.gz` or maybe `out.zip`. The drawback is it puts the burden on the client to have to manually uncompress the files they download.

dk's out.csv is 130MB. gzipped that's 26MB. (bzip2 is 24MB) us-ca-los_angeles_county goes from 126MB to 33MB. So we're talking about a ~75% space, bandwidth, and time savings.

We could extend this idea to other large products, particularly any `cache.json` or `cache.csv` files.

issue_title: ClientSession inside an aiohttp Server does not support connection pooling
issue_body:
I've got a aiohttp server that makes multiple http requests to other services in response to its inbound requests, I want to use a ClientSession for each inbound request to handle any cookie state that may be needed across the downstream requests it makes - but this means my server makes no use of connection-pooling/keep-alive when making downstream requests.

I could share a single ClientSession across all inbound requests, but then cookie/header state within the ClientSession would be re-used across inbound requests, which would not be correct. I could perhaps create a SharedClientSession object that handled switching between cookies/headers with a single underlying ClientSession, but that seems like I'm fighting the system.

Is there a supported way to do this? The most elegant way I can imagine would be a single ClientSession per inbound request with a global tcpconnector - except the ClientSession takes ownership and closes it, making it impossible to use in this way.

issue_title: ASGI support?
issue_body:
## Long story short Currently, most of asyncio based web frameworks embeds http server into the web framework. If http/ws servers would use ASGI standard, then it would be possible to run your app using different http/ws servers. That is why I think it is important for aiohttp to add ASGI support. ## Expected behaviour I would like to write aiohttp app: ```python from aiohttp import web async def hello(request): return web.Response(text="Hello world!") app = web.Application() app.add_routes([web.get('/', hello)]) ``` And run it with any ASGI compatible server: ``` > daphne app:app ``` ``` > http -b get :8080/ Hello world! ``` ## Actual behaviour ``` > daphne app:app 2018-04-02 12:48:51,097 ERROR Traceback (most recent call last): File "daphne/http_protocol.py", line 158, in process "server": self.server_addr, File "daphne/server.py", line 184, in create_application application_instance = self.application(scope=scope) TypeError: __call__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'scope' 127.0.0.1:41828 - - [02/Apr/2018:12:48:51] "GET /" 500 452 ``` ``` > http -b get :8080/ 500 Internal Server Error

500 Internal Server Error

Daphne HTTP processing error

Daphne
``` ## ASGI resources https://github.com/django/asgiref/blob/master/specs/asgi.rst - ASGI specification. https://github.com/django/asgiref/blob/master/specs/www.rst - ASGI-HTTP and ASGI-WebSocket protocol specifications. Example ASGI app: ```python import json def app(scope): async def channel(receive, send): message = await receive() if scope['method'] == 'POST': response = message else: response = scope await send({ 'type': 'http.response.start', 'status': 200, 'headers': [ [b'Content-Type', b'application/json'], ], }) await send({ 'type': 'http.response.body', 'body': json.dumps(response, default=bytes.decode).encode(), }) await send({ 'type': 'http.disconnect', }) return channel ``` ``` > daphne app:app 2018-03-31 22:28:10,823 INFO Starting server at tcp:port=8000:interface=127.0.0.1 2018-03-31 22:28:10,824 INFO HTTP/2 support enabled 2018-03-31 22:28:10,824 INFO Configuring endpoint tcp:port=8000:interface=127.0.0.1 2018-03-31 22:28:10,825 INFO Listening on TCP address 127.0.0.1:8000 127.0.0.1:43436 - - [31/Mar/2018:22:28:17] "GET /" 200 347 127.0.0.1:43440 - - [31/Mar/2018:22:28:22] "POST /" 200 43 127.0.0.1:43446 - - [31/Mar/2018:22:28:42] "POST /" 200 54 ``` ``` > http -b get :8000/ { "type": "http" "http_version": "1.1", "method": "GET", "path": "/", "query_string": "", "root_path": "", "scheme": "http", "headers": [ ["host", "localhost:8000"], ["user-agent", "HTTPie/0.9.9"], ["accept-encoding", "gzip, deflate"], ["accept", "*/*"], ["connection", "keep-alive"] ], "client": ["127.0.0.1", 43360], "server": ["127.0.0.1", 8000], } > http -b -f post :8000/ foo=bar { "body": "foo=bar", "type": "http.request" } > http -b -j post :8000/ foo=bar { "body": "{\"foo\": \"bar\"}", "type": "http.request" } ```

issue_title: StaticResource url versioning
issue_body:
Add feature for generating urls to static files with version hash to have more control over caching in browser.

## Expected behaviour
When you resolve url from `StaticResource` you can use `append_version` flag to add a hash of file (version) into url, which will change if file content changed and than for browser it's will be new url (which not exists in cache and will be reloaded)

## Example
```html
<script src="{{ url('static', filename='build/js/bundle.js', append_version=True) }}"></script>
```
with `append_version` flag `StaticResource` will append `v` param into url query string  with hash of the file
```
/static/build/js/bundle.js?v=aDc7UcFqFqXFUOYI8crlpxDAW-i41AGSNdDP8eQebSM%3D
```

issue_title: Simplify stream classes
issue_body:
- [x] drop `ChunksQueue` -- not used by aiohttp code
- [x] drop `FlowControlChunksQueue` -- not used by aiohttp code
- [x] Merge `FlowControlStreamReader` into `StreamReader`, drop `FlowControlStreamReader`. The reader without flow control is not used by aiohttp, `FlowControlStreamReader` is too long name. Getting rid of `await super()` calls may boost performance a little.
- [x] Add hysteresis to `StreamReader` to prevent flipping between paused and resumed states too often.

issue_title: Websocket do not close if use asyncio.wait to handle websocket
issue_body:
## Long story short
To design a websocket proxy I must create a websocket handler coroutine executed by asyncio.wait. 
To be more clear here is a simple example of the code:
```
import aiohttp
import asyncio

from aiohttp import web


async def handle_ws_message(ws):
    print("Bengin handle_ws_message")
    async for msg in ws:
        print(msg)
        if msg.type == aiohttp.WSMsgType.TEXT:
            break
    print("End handle_ws_message")


async def handle_request(request):
    print("Begin handle_request")
    ws = web.WebSocketResponse()
    await ws.prepare(request)
    print("WS ready")
    await asyncio.wait(
        [handle_ws_message(ws)],
        return_when=asyncio.FIRST_COMPLETED
    )
    print("End handle_request")
    return ws


app = web.Application()
app.router.add_route('*', '', handle_request)
web.run_app(app, port=8080)
```
## Expected / Actual behaviour

<!-- What is the behaviour you expect? -->

From google chrome on linux, if I open a websocket using :
```js
ws = new WebSocket("ws://127.0.0.1:8080")
ws.send('hello')
```
I obtain the expected result on my terminal:
```
======== Running on http://0.0.0.0:8080 ========
(Press CTRL+C to quit)
Begin handle_request
WS ready
Bengin handle_ws_message
WSMessage(type=<WSMsgType.TEXT: 1>, data='hello', extra='')
End handle_ws_message
End handle_request
```

But if I open a websocket from google chrome on linux using:
```js
ws = new WebSocket("ws://127.0.0.1:8080")
```
and then close the tabs, I observe on my terminal : 
```
Begin handle_request
WS ready
Bengin handle_ws_message
End handle_ws_message
```
instead of :
```
Begin handle_request
WS ready
Bengin handle_ws_message
End handle_ws_message
End handle_request
```

On that case, it seems that the coroutine never ended.
A bug maybe ?

## Your environment
Executed using linux ubuntu 16.04 (64-bit) on python 3.6 with aiohttp 2.0.5.
Navigator: Google chrome 58.0.3029.110 (64-bit)
<!-- Describe the environment you have that lead to your issue.
     This includes project version, OS, proxy server and other bits that
     are related to your case. -->

issue_title: Simplify formgrade
issue_body:
After looking through formgrade with the goal of updating it to be a JupyterHub 0.7.0 service. I have been trying to think through ways of simplifying formgrade. Some thoughts:

* Remove the ability of formgrade users to access live single user notebook servers. @minrk is working on an example of running a single user notebook server as a JupyterHub service with group level access (https://github.com/jupyterhub/jupyterhub/issues/921).
* This change would make it *much* easier to migrate formgrade to being a pretty stock JupyterHub service.
* I am also starting to explore replacing formgrade entirely by a live notebook server/jupyterlab with additional nbextensions/labextensions. Some initial ideas below...

The core client UI of formgrade could be replace in many situation by a live notebook with the following:

* An nbextension that allow human grading and commenting and stores the results in nbgrader metadata.
* A new nbgrader command line phase that takes that metadata and stores it in the db. We already have nbconvert preprocessors that essentially do this, so this would be super simple.
* Some sort of additional frontend UI that makes it easy to navigate through notebooks for grading.

In JupyterLab, offering additional UIs is relatively easy. I don't have a ton of time right now to work on this stuff, but I may pick away at it.

issue_title: Show Instructions when mail_draft/handover fail due to user setup missing.
issue_body:
When a user that isn't setup to do handover or mail_draft runs these commands it eventually errors out. We should instead print out a message telling them to email gcb-help.

The handover service setup process is manual and we currently have no good way of receiving the users's keys in a secure manner. What we have done so far is to have them put it into a shared computer where we have root access and can read it. The need to have their keys should go away once changes to DukeDS are implemented.

    load_entry_point('giraffez==1.1.2', 'console_scripts', 'giraffez')()
  File "/home/ec2-user/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/giraffez/__main__.py", line 25, in main

  File "/home/ec2-user/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/giraffez/core.py", line 91, in run

  File "/home/ec2-user/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/giraffez/commandline.py", line 147, in run
    log.write(MESSAGE_WRITE_DEFAULT.format(args.conf))
NameError: global name 'MESSAGE_WRITE_DEFAULT' is not defined
```

commandline.py (line 147) just needs to reference the new variable name from this commit b37054ed42b4683b05553e5fe67741339156eb4b:

From:

To:
`log.write(message_write_default.format(args.conf))`

# Visual Studio for Mac .NET Core 2.0 issues are no longer true with VS for Mac 7.1

[Known issues for .NET Core 2.0 document](https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/release-notes/2.0/2.0.0-known-issues.md) has a couple of references to Visual Studio for Mac which are out of date:

```
Visual Studio for the Mac

Visual Studio for the Mac 7.1 (build 1258) was recently released into beta and supports .NET Core 2.0 Preview 2.
```

Visual Studio for Mac 7.1 (build 1297) is now stable and supports .NET Core 2.0 RTM.

```
Visual Studio for Mac

There are a few items to be aware of if you are using .NET Core 2.0 with Visual Studio for Mac.
```

The above section links to a set of [issues](https://gist.github.com/mrward/70b8132003ef77d893111ecbea3e2225) which only apply to VS for Mac 7.0 so we should probably remove the above section.

issue_title: data-val-number is not added to int fields
issue_body:
As the title says, the unobtrusive data tag for int fields are not added to the html output, for example:

the view model class:
`public int Test { get; set; }` 

the razor view:
`<input asp-for="Test" class="form-control" />`

the html result:
`<input class="form-control" data-val="true" data-val-required="The Test field is required." id="Test" name="Test" value="" type="number">`

The problem is that now if I enter a text that is not a number, I'm getting the default jquery.validate message " Please enter a valid number. ", but if I manually add the data-val-number="some message", I get correctly the message I want.

Any thoughts?

thank you

issue_title: Support Console.beep on Linux (Fedora/RHEL)
issue_body:
Run on OS: Fedora 27  
Kernel Linux:  4.13.16-302.fc27.x86_64  

Step zero: `yum install beep`  
First, run` sudo modprobe pcspkr` and then beep should work.
  
Additional uncomment the /etc/modprobe.d/beep.conf line at:
`# alias platform:pcspkr pcspkr`
to
`alias platform:pcspkr pcspkr`
For automatic load pcspkr module  

 Run application beep e.g -  `/usr/bin/beep  -f 2000 -l 1000 ` 
 The beep in this case equal to n = 2000 Hz and duration 1000 ms

This is tiny, small C source includes, of beep application. 
 https://goo.gl/E3kfPy  

The portion of this code can be re-write to C# and use in Console.beep implementation for Linux platform.

issue_body:
# Kestrel ListenUnixSocket throws `Only ListenType.IPEndPoint is supported` as of 2.1 RC

# General

After upgrading to .NET Core 2.1 RC, I am no longer able to use kestrel's 'ListenUnixSocket' feature to use unix sockets instead of TCP sockets.

## Stack Trace
Unhandled Exception: System.ArgumentException: Only ListenType.IPEndPoint is supported.

   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.Internal.WebHost.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.WebHostExtensions.RunAsync(IWebHost host, CancellationToken token, String shutdownMessage)
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.WebHostExtensions.RunAsync(IWebHost host, CancellationToken token)
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.WebHostExtensions.Run(IWebHost host)
   at SampleNamespace.Website.Program.Main(String[] args) in C:\BuildAgent.VSTS\_work\26\s\SampleNamespace.Website\Program.cs:line 18
```

## Code
```CSharp
  WebHost.CreateDefaultBuilder(args)
#if DEBUG
                .UseEnvironment("Development")
#elif STAGING
                .UseEnvironment("Staging")
#else
                .UseEnvironment("Production")
                .UseApplicationInsights()
#endif
                .UseStartup<Startup>()
                .UseKestrel(options =>
                {
                    if (Environment.OSVersion.Platform == PlatformID.Unix)
                    {
                        options.ListenUnixSocket("/tmp/kestrel.sock");

                    //options.Listen(IPAddress.Loopback, 8080);
                })
                .Build();
```

## Extra Info
**OS:** Ubuntu 16.04.4
**.NET Core Version:** 2.1.300-rc1-008673
**Project Packages:** All updated to v2.1.0-rc1-final

Would this have anything to do with Kestrel's update in 2.1 where HTTPs is the default while debugging?

issue_title: Add support for setup_tasks in frigg.yml

issue_title: Run configtest before attempting to modify config files
issue_body: It's possible that the user's config files are in a bad state before we even touch them. We should run `service nginx configtest` or equivalent before even trying to modify configs.

issue_title: flatten layer could inheret colormap and minmax from previous layer(s)
Switch builds over to using tox
issue_body:
The way I've got the builds in travis and appveyor set up is messing with the coverage metrics (see #528).

Given that we're already maintaining a `tox.ini` (which is not actually automatically tested, mind you!), it's probably a good idea to switch over the builds to use `tox`.

Rather than just have a single build that runs `tox`, it's probably best to use `tox -e <environment>` in the `script`. Once we're using tox, we can also add a `changedir` directive in the tox environments so that the tests are run against the installed version.

issue_title: Set default arguments in cwltool.factory.Factory
issue_body: In the README, the recommended way to use cwltool as a factory is to use the `cwltool.factory.Factory` class (https://github.com/common-workflow-language/cwltool#import-as-a-module). However, this doesn't set default command line options - these are normally set in the command line using argparse in main by: https://github.com/common-workflow-language/cwltool/blob/f691bf1aa66f2836694716c9845731962147079b/cwltool/main.py#L756 If no default command line options are passed when instantiating the Factory, then this produces lots of unintended effects (e.g. use_container is False)

issue_title: CKEDITOR_RESTRICT_BY_USER using a possibly mutable value
issue_body:
Currently if you use `CKEDITOR_RESTRICT_BY_USER` to divide files according to users, it's done by using the username.  This may be fine for a lot of people, but there's nothing in Django that doesn't allow you to modify the username on a person.  So, if a person changes their username, all of their uploaded files will become orphaned.  Even worse, if another user changes their username to one previously used by another user then they'll gain access to those uploaded files.

#178 would allow using the `id` value which is not mutable.  Obviously that PR isn't suitable as-is, but something like that is needed if people are using a mutable "username" (or whatever is returned by `get_username()`).

issue_title: Implement event submission form
issue_body:
We need to add a public user-facing form that people can use to submit events to our platform. We should be able to implement this entirely with Django's built-in form utilities. 

To start, you'll want to add three new files to the `dispatch/apps/events` directory: `views.py`, `forms.py` and `urls.py`.

You'll then want to update `dispatch/urls.py` to include the events URLs (similar to what's being done for the API urls).

In the `events/urls.py` file, create a new URL (maybe "submit"?) that connects to a submit view defined in `views.py`

You'll also want to add a new directory `dispatch/apps/events/templates` that contains a single template named "submit.html". You'll use this later to render the form layout.

Read through Django's documentation on forms for a good starting place: https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/topics/forms/

issue_title: dump_only whitelist/inverse / load_only whilst including when dumping
issue_body:
I feel like this kind of touches on #375, but that didn't seem to align with the behaviour that I think many would find useful. Also highly related to #61 

It would be useful to use the meta class (or constructor) to be able to specify a set of fields that are whitelisted for deserialisation. If the field doesn't exist in the iterable, it cannot be deserialised. 

If a user of marshmallow had a large schema class, with many fields, but only wanted 1 of those fields to be deserializable/writable, from my understanding they could either:

- name every field they want as read only in `dump_only` attribute (lots of fields, prone to errors, ie missing a field)
- When loading data, specify `only=('fieldname',)` upon instantiating the schema. (prone to errors, forgetting to put this in when instantiating)

Am I missing something, or is this a shortcoming that could be addressed? 

Also to note, the naming of `load_only` feels like a misnomer without reading the docs in detail - If you specify a field in load_only, it will only be available for loading, not dumping - but taking another perspective to this could imply that it's an iterable of fields that are whitelisted to be loaded, but can still be dumped.

issue_title: read() semantics are broken
issue_body:
Currently, a call to `read(x)` reads _exactly_ x bytes. If less than x bytes are available, pyserial waits until an I/O timeout occurs. That behavior is comparable to doing file I/O. The operating system will do its best to read _exactly_ x bytes.

However, a serial port is not a file. A serial port gives you access to two byte streams, one for reading, one for writing, very much similar to a TCP socket. If you look closely how you read from TCP sockets in python, you will find that a socket's recv(x) function returns _at most_ x bytes. In fact, it blocks until at least one byte is in the buffer. Then the recv(x) function will return as much data as possible, but never more than x bytes. A read timeout will only occur, if no bytes are received for a given amount of time.

recv()-like semantics can be implemented with pyserial,  but it's cumbersome:
```
def recv(s, x):
  data = s.read(1)
  return data+s.read(min(x-1, s.in_waiting))
```

This solution avoids polling in_waiting which would clearly waste CPU time.

That pyserial doesn't offer a recv()-like function to read from a serial port is poor design. An application reading from a serial port relies on recv()-like semantics as it usually does not know how many bytes it can expect to read next.

issue_title: Read-only mode is broken for old loom files and should be checked for in more functions
issue_body:
# Read-only mode and missing HDF5 attributes/groups

After switching to loompy 2, old loom files suddenly refused to open in the loom-viewer. Trying to open them in the REPL caused no problems, and after doing so they could suddenly be opened by the loom-viewer too!

The cause turned out to be that when an older file misses recent additions such as graphs or timestamps, loompy creates a default value for these, [as seen in this section of `GraphManager.__init__`](https://github.com/linnarsson-lab/loompy/blob/9049f9d07e1dd6f50a7694da5337738fed0d768a/loompy/graph_manager.py#[L22-L34]):

```python
a = ["row_edges", "col_edges"][self.axis]
if a in ds._file:
	for key in ds._file[a]:
		self.__dict__["storage"][key] = None
else:
	ds._file.create_group(a)
```

HDF5 (rightfully) complains if you try to do this in read-only mode:
```python
  File "h5py/_objects.pyx", line 54, in h5py._objects.with_phil.wrapper
  File "h5py/_objects.pyx", line 55, in h5py._objects.with_phil.wrapper
  File "h5py/h5g.pyx", line 151, in h5py.h5g.create
ValueError: Unable to create group (no write intent on file)
```

This incidentally also explains why the cache did not work as expected: if `last_modified` is missing, the most recent timestamp is returned, meaning the cache is *always* outdated. As a work-around I am writing an "initiate_last_modified" function that opens a loom file with write-access, iterates over over *all possible time-stamp elements* to ensure they are initialised, then closes, after which the loom-viewer can resume with using read-only mode.

# Adding read-only mode checks to all methods that modify loom files

Currently, only the `last_modified` methods, as well as `add_columns` and `add_loom` check for write access. I suggest we extend this to *all* setters and similar functions that modify the loom file (like `permute`). We cannot prevent people from bypassing the loompy library and modifying the HDF5 file, anyone respecting the library API would at least have better protection this way.

To make this easy to implement, and to ensure that this does not notably slow down regular `r+` usage, we can add a private boolean to `LoomConnection` during `__init__`:

```python
# LoomConnection
self._write_access = mode == 'r+'

# All helper classes that are passed a LoomConnection as `ds`
self._write_access = ds._write_access
```

Then we just have to add these two lines to all functions that modify the loom file:
```python
if not self._write_access:
	raise IOError("Cannot modify loom file when connected in read-only mode")
```

The added benefit of this would be that we raise an error before going all the way down to the h5py or HDF5 library, reducing reliance on *those* implementation of being bug-free.

issue_title: "Adding Search Functionality" in cookbook is overcomplicated and missing functionality
issue_body:
The current code

```
paginator = Paginator(sqs, 20)

try:
    page = paginator.page(int(request.GET.get('page', 1)))
except InvalidPage:
    raise Http404("Sorry, no results on that page.")

objects = []

for result in page.object_list:
    bundle = self.build_bundle(obj=result.object, request=request)
    bundle = self.full_dehydrate(bundle)
    objects.append(bundle)

object_list = {
    'objects': objects,
}

self.log_throttled_access(request)
return self.create_response(request, object_list)
```

is wrong, in my opinion.  It both hard-codes pages of size 20 and uses the default Paginator in a dated way.  This means that neither `limit` nor `offset` work.  Also it doesn't have a `meta` attribute, and it is unnecessarily long and confusing.

Instead, it should look a good deal like `get_list`:

```
    paginator = self._meta.paginator_class(request.GET, sqs,
       resource_uri=self.get_resource_uri(), limit=self._meta.limit,
       max_limit=self._meta.max_limit, collection_name=self._meta.collection_name)

    to_be_serialized = paginator.page()

    bundles = [self.build_bundle(obj=o.object, request=request) for o in to_be_serialized['objects']]
    to_be_serialized['objects'] = [self.full_dehydrate(bundle) for bundle in bundles]
    to_be_serialized = self.alter_list_data_to_serialize(request, to_be_serialized)
    return self.create_response(request, to_be_serialized)
```

issue_title: Automated testing
issue_body:
I have made a pull request https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/pull/66 in which I included automated testing. A few notes regarding the PR:

1- I have used `pytest` as the test tool. It's better imo than Django Testing. The original tests have not been touched as `py.test` is compatible with them. Nonetheless, if you don't like it, automation can be done without it and I can edit the PR (see the examples on the pytest [website](http://pytest.org/latest/contents.html))

2- `pytest` is able to generate a `runtests` script which I did, so `python runtests.py` would run the tests without the user invoking `py.test` manually.

3- `Tox` has been used to test different environments. It's a great tool for such purposes and it goes well with `pytest` but it's not a necessary. (see [docs](https://tox.readthedocs.org/en/latest/)).

Now this is the worse part:

4- If we're to test a combination of `python2.7`, `python3.2`, `python3.3`, `python3.4`, `django1.6`, `django1.7`, `django1.8`, `drf2.4`, `drf3.0`, `drf3.1`, `drf3.2` Then only a handful of these tests will pass. Anything other than `python{27,33,34}, django18, drf{31,32}` will FAIL. I had a quick look at the failures, some of them are easy to fix and some are not. However, I didn't fix anything in order to follow a *branch per feature* process.

Finally, thanks to [drf-json-api](https://github.com/kevin-brown/drf-json-api) as I got the idea for automated testing from it. That project is **identical** to this but this is more mature and active and the code is quite clean. Sad fragmentation in OSS.

issue_title: grad_clipping clips gate activations, not gradient
issue_body:
I don't understand the way grad_clipping is implemented in the recurrent layers. Instead of clipping gradients, gate activations get clipped. Is there a reason for this? 1. I don't know any publication doing this and 2. it doesn't solve the exploding gradient problem.

The correct implementation should clip the gradient and it therefore should be implemented in the update functions.

Also you use theano.gradient.grad_clip, which works on each parameter independently (= hard clipping). Consider limiting the L2 norm of the gradient to a certain value, dividing each parameter by the same value and thus preserving the correct directiong of the gradient.
See [On the difficulty of training Recurrent Neural Networks](http://www.jmlr.org/proceedings/papers/v28/pascanu13.pdf), Algorithm 1. 

I use it the following way: 
(copied from https://github.com/kastnerkyle/minet/blob/master/minet/net.py#L236 )
```
def sgd_with_grad_clipping(loss_or_grads, params, learning_rate, rescale):
    grads = lasagne.updates.get_or_compute_grads(loss_or_grads, params)
    updates = OrderedDict()

    grad_norm = T.sqrt(sum(map(lambda x: T.sqr(x).sum(), grads)))
    not_finite = T.or_(T.isnan(grad_norm), T.isinf(grad_norm))
    grad_norm = T.sqrt(grad_norm)
    scaling_num = rescale
    scaling_den = T.maximum(rescale, grad_norm)
    for n, (param, grad) in enumerate(zip(params, grads)):
        grad = T.switch(not_finite, 0.1 * param,
            grad * (scaling_num / scaling_den))
        updates[param] = param - learning_rate * grad
    return updates   
```

issue_title: `CustomRecurrentLayer` does not allow hidden-to-hidden sub-net to have arbitrary depth
issue_body:
When using a `CustomRecurrentLayer` the hidden-to-hidden network can only have one layer besides the input, unless the second last layer has the same output dimensions as output shape. Here is some code based on the unit tests that triggers the bug:

```py
    # Check that the custom recurrent layer can handle a hidden-to-hidden
    # network with an arbitrary depth
    n_batch, n_steps, n_channels, width, height = (2, 3, 4, 5, 6)
    n_out_filters = 7
    n_in_hid_filters_0 = 11
    n_hid_hid_filters_0 = 13
    filter_shape = (3, 3)
    l_in = lasagne.layers.InputLayer(
        (n_batch, n_steps, n_channels, width, height))

    # Expect the output shape of `l_in` as input shape for input-to-hidden
    l_in_to_hid = lasagne.layers.InputLayer((None, n_channels, width, height))
    # Two conv layers; first to `n_hid_filters_0` channels
    l_in_to_hid = lasagne.layers.Conv2DLayer(
        l_in_to_hid, n_in_hid_filters_0, filter_shape, pad='same')
    # then to `n_out_filters` channels
    l_in_to_hid = lasagne.layers.Conv2DLayer(
        l_in_to_hid, n_out_filters, filter_shape, pad='same')

    # Expect the output shape of `l_in_to_hid` as input shape for
    # hidden-to-hidden
    l_hid_to_hid = lasagne.layers.InputLayer((None, n_out_filters,
                                              width, height))
    # Two conv layers; first to `n_hid_hid_filters_0` channels
    l_hid_to_hid = lasagne.layers.Conv2DLayer(
        l_hid_to_hid, n_hid_hid_filters_0, filter_shape, pad='same')
    # then to `n_out_filters` channels
    l_hid_to_hid = lasagne.layers.Conv2DLayer(
        l_hid_to_hid, n_out_filters, filter_shape, pad='same')

    # --- ValueError raised HERE ---
    l_rec = lasagne.layers.CustomRecurrentLayer(
        l_in, l_in_to_hid, l_hid_to_hid)

    assert l_rec.output_shape == (n_batch, n_steps, n_out_filters, width,
                                  height)

    out = theano.function([l_in.input_var], lasagne.layers.get_output(l_rec))
    out_shape = out(np.zeros((n_batch, n_steps, n_channels, width, height),
                             dtype=theano.config.floatX)).shape
    assert out_shape == (n_batch, n_steps, n_out_filters, width, height)
```

issue_title: Issue may cause headache, networkx.DiGraph() and add_edge
issue_body:
Hello Guys, 

I am reporting something I stumbled upon today and before starting with the Issue: This may not concern you at all, but from my experience with angr's  CFGFast / .. from over a year ago it seemed it may be an issue. 

The problem lies within how networkx.DiGraph() handles the add_edge(node_1, node_2) method and I am trying to describe the problem as simple as possible. I first encountered the problem when loading a serialized networkx.DiGraph() from a database, the core problem is the following code:

```
sit_cfg_abstraction.function_cfg_dict[graphstartnodeaddr].add_edge(CFGNode(fromaddr, fromsize, sit_cfg_abstraction),
                                                            CFGNode(toaddr, tosize, sit_cfg_abstraction))
```

The problem already occurs with a linear chain of blocks, lets say 3 blocks with the relationship (succ, pred) leading to a code like this:
```
n1 <--> n2 <--> n3
sit_cfg_abstraction.function_cfg_dict[graphstartnodeaddr].add_edge(CFGNode(0x00, 4, sit_cfg_abstraction),
                                                            CFGNode(0x4, 4, sit_cfg_abstraction))

sit_cfg_abstraction.function_cfg_dict[graphstartnodeaddr].add_edge(CFGNode(0x04, 4, sit_cfg_abstraction),
                                                            CFGNode(0x8, 4, sit_cfg_abstraction))

```

Despite creating the CFGNode for addr 0x4 two times, leading to different id(..)'s, the creating networkx.DiGraph holds only 3 nodes, because existing nodes (by __hash__) won't be added to DiGraph.nodes. So far, this is what it's supposed to be. 

However, for further simplification, above code can be written as:

```
n1 <--> n2 <--> n3
sit_cfg_abstraction.function_cfg_dict[graphstartnodeaddr].add_edge(n1, n2)
sit_cfg_abstraction.function_cfg_dict[graphstartnodeaddr].add_edge(x2, n3)

with __hash__(n2) == __hash__(x2)
```

with x2 never ending in the graph.nodes dict. However, x2 makes it way to the predecessor relationships of the graph, without being in the nodes dictionary. Let's take a look into the networkx code:

```
add_edge(self, u, v, ..):
       ...
        if u not in self.succ:
            self.succ[u]= self.adjlist_dict_factory()
            self.pred[u]= self.adjlist_dict_factory()
            self.node[u] = {}
        if v not in self.succ:
            self.succ[v]= self.adjlist_dict_factory()
            self.pred[v]= self.adjlist_dict_factory()
            self.node[v] = {}
        # add the edge
        datadict=self.adj[u].get(v,self.edge_attr_dict_factory())
        datadict.update(attr_dict)
        self.succ[u][v]=datadict
        self.pred[v][u]=datadict
```

The first iteration leads to:

```
sit_cfg_abstraction.function_cfg_dict[graphstartnodeaddr].add_edge(n1, n2)
leading to:
node = {n1, n2}
succ = {n1 -> n2, n2 -> {} }
pred = {n1 -> {}, n2 -> n1}
```

But the problem comes with the second iteration
```
sit_cfg_abstraction.function_cfg_dict[graphstartnodeaddr].add_edge(x2, n3)
leading to:
node = {n1, n2, n3}  #(if x2 not in self.succ: is false, since hash(x2) = hash(n2) and n2 is in the list) 
succ = {n1 -> n2, n2 -> n3, n3 -> {}} #self.succ[x2][n3] = .. works, since hash(x2) == hash(n2) 
pred = {n1 -> {}, n2 -> n1, n3 -> x2} #self.pred[n3][x2] = .. problem
```

And here we finally arrived at the problem. Whenever I want to get graph.predecessors(n3), i will receive x2 which is not in the graph in the first place. More problematic, altering the nodes in the graph has no effect for predecessor nodes. And by all means, there are duplicate nodes which consume memory. 

So, I can fix this on application side - and maybe you guys did it in some way, didn't check it thoroughly - but maybe this is also an unnoticed Issue for you. In my opinion, this is a general networkx problem and should be addressed there with something like:

```
if u not in self.succ:
            self.succ[u]= self.adjlist_dict_factory()
            self.pred[u]= self.adjlist_dict_factory()
            self.node[u] = {}
else:
            u = self.node[u]
```

Tell me when you stopped reading ;)

issue_title: Merge simuvex and angr
issue_body:
# Current Status

SimuVEX is no more. It's been absorbed into angr, and lives on as a compatibility stub. Path is gone, and SimulationManager is the new PathGroup. The future is here.

# The Plan

The current plan is to take this in 3 phases:

* [x] Phase 1: merge simuvex into angr, redistribute the files under the angr module. No functionality changes.
  * [x] angr passes
  * [x] angr-bf passes
  * [x] angr-doc passes
  * [x] tracer passes
  * [x] rex passes
  * [x] colorguard passes
  * [x] identifier passes
  * [x] fidget passes
  * [x] patcherex passes
  * [x] angrop passes
* [x] Phase 2: remove Path, make PathHistory a SimStatePlugin called StateHistory, rename PathHierarchy to StateHierarchy, rename PathGroup to SimContext.
  * [x] angr passes
  * [x] angr-bf passes
  * [x] angr-doc passes
  * [x] tracer passes
  * [ ] rex passes
  * [x] colorguard passes
  * [x] identifier passes
  * [x] fidget passes
  * [x] patcherex passes
  * [x] angrop passes
* [x] Phase 3: full backwards compatibility -- stub modules for `simuvex`, `angr.Path`, and alias for `angr.PathGroup`
  * [x] simuvex stub
  * [ ] pass-throughs for state.log and state.scratch
  * [x] alias for PathGroup
  * [x] alias for `state.state`
  * [ ] alias for `state.length`
  * [ ] old angr-doc examples pass
  * [ ] old angr testcases pass
  * [ ] old angr-bf testcases pass
  * [ ] old tracer passes
  * [ ] old rex passes
  * [ ] old colorguard passes
  * [ ] old identifier passes
  * [ ] old fidget passes
  * [ ] old patcherex passes
  * [ ] old angrop passes
* [ ] Phase 4: clean up remaining issues in the SimuVEX repo.
* [ ] Phase 5: squash angr commits to make Fish happy

# Original Musings

@rhelmot and I had a discussion that started out with my frustration that adding a syscall to angr requires two PRs (one to SimuVEX for the SimProcedure and one for angr to change the SimOS syscall table). @rhelmot made an unorthodox suggestion that I'll reiterate toward the end of this issue. First, some observations:

1. Some of the modules we've written for angr are being used in other projects. This includes CLE, PyVEX, claripy, and archinfo. One module conspicuously missing from this list (as far as I know) is SimuVEX.
2. This is probably because the other modules have very well-defined, self-contained purposes, separate from angr. SimuVEX, on the other hand, lives as a tightly-coupled state mutation engine for angr (you can probably begin to see where this is going now).
3. On the angr side, over the last year, `Path` has become and more and more thin wrapper around `SimState`, `PathHistory`, and `CallStack` (the last of which really belongs in `PathHistory`, anyways), and convenience accessors for those (i.e., `path.events`, `path.actions`, `path.jumpkind`, and the like are all pass-throughs to data stored in `PathHistory`).
4. State options, while technically a SimuVEX construct and technically tied to the state, is used to control behavior all over the place in angr (i.e., in the CFG, in `PathHistory`, in `PathHierarchy`, etc).
5. SimInspect breakpoints, also technically a SimuVEX construct, have all sorts of angr-triggered events.
6. We're constantly running into problems where we don't have access to the path or project from SimuVEX (specifically, from `SimInspect` hooks).
7. Because we run into the same problem tracking information over successive paths as we do over successive states, we've invented `path.info`, which is basically a stripped down `SimStatePlugin`.
8. We've had debates on, for example, pulling SimProcedures out of SimuVEX, pulling SimOS out of angr and into some weird joint package with the SimProcedures, etc.
9. SimEngines are currently split across two different packages: `SimEngineVEX`, `SimEngineProcedure`, and `SimEngineUnicorn` are in SimuVEX, `SimEngineFailure` and `SimEngineSyscall` are in angr.
10. Simu**VEX** is a weird name for a state mutation engine that supports multiple execution/translation engines.

Basically, the decoupling between angr and simuvex is not really there, unlike it is with CLE, PyVEX, claripy, archinfo, etc. A proposed solution is to **eliminate simuvex itself, and merge it into angr**. The changes would be:

1. SimuVEX would be merged into the angr repo.
2. `Path` would be eliminated. Each `SimState` would have a `state.history` attribute with the `PathHistory` (now probably called `StateHistory`). This would eliminate the weird thing where we copy in everything from the `state.log` plugin into `path.history` every state -- they could be the same entity.
3. `PathGroup` would now wrangle stashes of states instead of stashes of paths.

To make the transition smoother, we'd have the following deprecation support:

1. If we create a `state.state` property, people can still do stuff like `path_groups.found[0].state` as before. Likewise for properties for `state.history` (which would be the replacement of `state.log`, anyways), `state.callstack`, and so on.
2. We'd probably have to leave a dummy simuvex package for a while, with deprecated passthroughs to the old concepts.

This is a big conceptual change -- it's angr 7.7.4.1 sort of stuff for sure, but it seems to me that it would make sense and would simplify using and extending angr.

issue_title: Pandas categorical optimization
issue_body:
Datashader issue https://github.com/bokeh/datashader/issues/396 revealed a bottleneck in [odo/backends/pandas.py]() on this line: https://github.com/blaze/odo/blob/master/odo/backends/pandas.py#L22 . One culprit seems to be the `.tolist()` operation; here is my proposed fix:

```
    if isinstance(col.dtype, type(pd.Categorical.dtype)):
        cat_dshape = dshape('{} * {}'.format(
            len(col.cat.categories),
            col.cat.categories.dtype,
        ))
        return Categorical(
            col.cat.categories.values,
            type=cat_dshape,
            ordered=col.cat.categorical.ordered
        )
   ...
```

There is potential for an additional bottleneck to occur inside `datashape`'s `Categorical` constructor here: https://github.com/blaze/datashape/blob/master/datashape/coretypes.py#L1106 -
 where the first argument is coerced into a `tuple`. It seems like a simple refactor to drop the type coercion, but a symmetrical refactor to the `dshape()` functionality is necessary to get `datashape`'s unit tests to pass.

issue_title: pytest 3 breaks `test_ssh.py` test module
issue_body:
Running with `pytest=3.0.3` (which is currently installed by default in the travis build) I get the following

```
$ pytest odo/backends/tests/test_ssh.py 
======================================================== test session starts =========================================================
platform darwin -- Python 2.7.12, pytest-3.0.3, py-1.4.31, pluggy-0.4.0
rootdir: /Users/rqa267/blaze/odo, inifile: 
collected 0 items / 1 errors 

=============================================================== ERRORS ===============================================================
__________________________________________ ERROR collecting odo/backends/tests/test_ssh.py ___________________________________________
Using @pytest.skip outside of a test (e.g. as a test function decorator) is not allowed. Use @pytest.mark.skip or @pytest.mark.skipif instead.
!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Interrupted: 1 errors during collection !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
```

This is caused by the recent release of pytest 3, which breaks the use of `pytest.skip` imperatively outside of a test function, as discussed in pytest-dev/pytest#1959

This can be fixed in the build by installing `'pytest<3'`, in `.travis.yml`, but I would need to go through an approval process to submit a new PR, so I'll leave it to some other intrepid soul.

issue_title: JPEG does not support transparency, thus the user needs to specify a fill-value
issue_body:
#### Code Sample, a minimal, complete, and verifiable piece of code

```python
from satpy.scene import Scene
from satpy.utils import debug_on
debug_on()
from glob import glob

FILES = glob("/data/lang/satellit/geo/hrit/201610/20161019_12/H-000*__")
scn = Scene(reader="hrit_msg", filenames=FILES)
scn.load(['IR_108'])
scn.save_dataset('IR_108', './test_bw.jpg')
```

#### Problem description
JPEG does not support transparency, thus the user needs to specify a fill-value. But if the user does not remember to do so, as he/she may not actually know that what is about to be stored is an image with an alpha channel. The default in satpy is to have fill_value=None, and then the above code will result in the error listed below.

In the case of Satpy it is sufficient to do:
```
scn.save_dataset('IR_108', './test_bw.jpg', fill_value=0)
```

issue_title: Fix master index flushing
issue_body:
Some users are ending up in a very bad situation with many, many jobs causing Ganga to be unusable due to extremely long startup times caused by having to load many index files from disk.
This is exacerbated by events such as user hitting Ctrl+C or dropped connections and the master index not being re-generated correctly until shutdown.

At the moment all we do is remove it upon startup but it would be good to update the master index once every few min or so at least or update a version in memory to flush to disk with the jobs synchronisation so that future startups aren't effected by this bug.

issue_title: SplitByFiles(ignoremissing=False) seemingly ignored
issue_body:
Hi,

I just tried to submit a job with the following splitter options:
```
In [260]:jobs(4094).splitter
Out[260]: SplitByFiles (
 filesPerJob = 100 ,
 maxFiles = None ,
 bulksubmit = False ,
 ignoremissing = False
 )
```

I happened (fortunately, it could easily have been lost in the noise..) to notice:
```
WARNING  LFN: /lhcb/validation/Collision12/SWIMSTRIPPINGD02KSHH.MDST/00042039/0000/00042039_00008474_1.swimstrippingd02kshh.mdst was either unknown to DIRAC or unavailable, Ganga is ignoring it!
```
along with a few other messages along the same lines. I'm using 6.1.13 as it's the most recent release that seems somewhat usable with Tasks.

This file has a single replica, which was not available when I submitted the job, but I had hoped that having ```ignoremissing = False``` would mean that I got some kind of fatal error (or, preferably, a few subjobs that wouldn't be able to execute successfully until the site came back online).
Am I misunderstanding what the ignoremissing option is supposed to do?
It seems that if I hadn't spotted the warning then everything could have "succeeded" despite some fraction of the input data having been ignored, which is not good.

issue_title: How to edit reverse many to many fields?
issue_body:
Hi,

In the following case, i'm looking how to add the reverse edit form. I came a long way, but i'm missing a few things...

Model:
```
# Url <--> Organization

# models.py
class Url(models.Model):
    organization = models.ManyToManyField(Organization, related_name="u_many_o_upgrade")

#admin.py
class UrlAdmin(ActionMixin, ImportExportModelAdmin, admin.ModelAdmin):
   ...
    list_filter = (... 'organization')

    fieldsets = (
        (None, {
            'fields': ('organization', ...)
        }),
```
This results in an awesome edit field in Url:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/333846/37360263-022ff30c-26f0-11e8-8c24-5f870d5abf06.png)

The other way around seems impossible: which is a shame, since the input really works well. It's also understandable. Luckily editing related urls is possible using an inline:
```
class UrlAdminInline(CompactInline):
    model = Url.organization.through
    extra = 0
    show_change_link = True
```

Which results in:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/333846/37360562-f21967d6-26f0-11e8-8d89-9b465f1791e6.png)

This requires some customizations, and that is where i'm not able to find a solution.

The issues with the inline are:

1: The name of related objects are not presented via __str__, due to the many-to-many table being auto generated(?). Where to add the __str__ representation for each object?

2: The complete list of URLs is long, luckily there is an autocomplete feature in Jet. How to use autocomplete on the form that is generated?:
http://jet.readthedocs.io/en/latest/autocomplete.html?highlight=many

I hope someone can point me to the right direction. In the meanwhile i'll keep looking for answers.

Where should i look to solve above challenges?

issue_title: Lack of communication with serviceworkers
issue_body:
## Expected Behavior
A page should be able to communicate with its registered service worker via [postMessage](https://w3c.github.io/ServiceWorker/#serviceworker-interface) and or a [Message Channel](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/web-messaging.html#message-channels).

Likewise it is expected that the registered service worker can communicate with its controlled clients via [postMessage](https://w3c.github.io/ServiceWorker/#client-postmessage) or a [message port](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/web-messaging.html#messageport) on  a [message event](https://w3c.github.io/ServiceWorker/#extendablemessageevent-interface).

For example consider the following live web page: https://n0tan3rd.github.io/replay_test 
It registers a service worker and attempts to communicate with it via postMessage and a message channel
- [page js src](https://github.com/N0taN3rd/replay_test/blob/master/src/epics/swEpics.js)
- [service worker src](https://github.com/N0taN3rd/replay_test/blob/master/replayTest_SW.js)

## Actual Behavior
https://webrecorder.io/jberlin/sw/20180510171123/https://n0tan3rd.github.io/replay_test/
![serviceworkerbadrw](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8648317/40187437-8172888e-59c5-11e8-976b-2e1ff4af316e.png)

The current strategy employed by Pywb does supply postMessage rewrite fix for service workers 
https://github.com/webrecorder/pywb/blob/9acad278011e7ec6e2b4e2044e137d5d5382c840/pywb/rewrite/regex_rewriters.py#L160-L164

## Steps to Reproduce the Problem
Replay https://webrecorder.io/jberlin/sw/20180510171123/https://n0tan3rd.github.io/replay_test/

## Context
- Pywb Version:  2.0.3

issue_title: "Hint" Entity requirement
issue_body:
Currently, intent parsers support optional arguments, required arguments, and "at least one of these" requirements. Something that would help clean up the contents of a resolved intent would be the concept of a "hint" in the parser.

A hint would be an optional entity, but would not show up in the parsed intent. It would still be reflected in the intent confidence, so that the parse result would still rank appropriately. Any consumers of Adapt who depend on parse result confidence being the sum of all entity confidences (which is the case today, and not exposed via the engine or IntentParser.validate APIs) could be broken. The seems like an edge case, and folks breaking the abstraction layer should expect nastiness down the road :smile: 

This will require changes to IntentBuilder and IntentParser, as well as corresponding unit tests.

Example:
```
Imagine an intent for reminders.
IntentBuilder("CreateReminderIntent")
    .requires("DateTime", "ReminderTime") # datetime entity, arrival TBD
    .requires("ReminderContent") # regex entity
    .hint("ReminderKeyword")
```

Where ReminderKeyword is words/phrases like "reminder", "remind me to", etc.

issue_title: A limitation of Mutation?
Help text for arguments
Replace `-` with `_` when user passes "extra_arguments"
issue_body:
When using an explicity-defined cli opt, argparse replaces `-` with `_` by default. We are also accepting arbitrary cli opts into the `extra_arguments` list, which argparse does not munge. For the user, this means currently they would need to do this:

```
watchmaker --content_source <url.zip>
```

which doesn't match the structure of every other cli opt. Instead, we should munge `extra_arguments` to support the same structure as regular cli opts:

```
watchmaker --content-source <url.zip>
```

issue_title: Pipeline ignores missing files
issue_body:
Let's say I've misspelled a js file path in our 

```
PIPELINE_JS = {
    'my_js': {
        'source_filenames': (
            'existing_file.js',
            'wrong/path/to/some_important_file.js',
        ),
        'output_filename': 'my_js.min.js',
    },
}
```
this line
`if staticfiles_storage.exists(pathname):`
in `glob.iglob`
returns nothing in case the path does not exist. This makes the path being silently ignored in `packager.Package.sources`.
I don't know if this is a desired behavior, but going silent on configuration errors can create a lot of problems in production. My suggestion is to at least be able to control this behavior, e.g. with a setting that lets you decide if you want these errors to be notified.

issue_title: mmap files are treated as streams but can't be read
issue_body:
`xlrd` can operate on mmap files, so it would be useful to be able to pass in one to pyexcel, e.g.
```
sheet = pyexcel.get_sheet(file_type='xls', file_content=mmap.mmap(fp.fileno(), 0,
    access=mmap.ACCESS_READ))
```
`isstream` is true for an mmap because it has a read function, and so even though I passed in `file_content`, pyexcel-io's `get_data` calls `load_data` with `file_stream` rather than `file_content`. But this then means that down in pyexcel-xls, either `getvalue` is called (current release) which errors, or `read` is called without argument (after https://github.com/pyexcel/pyexcel-xls/issues/16's fix) which errors as mmap's read must have an argument.

My aim is to not have to read in file contents in one go at all anywhere, and for pyexcel-xls/xlrd, mmap appears to be the only way (and https://github.com/pyexcel/pyexcel-io/issues/33 would fix it for CSV I think). What I have done is create an `mmap` subclass that does not have a read function, which then means, pyexcel-io passes file_content through to pyexcel-xls and thus xlrd.

issue_title: Problems with pole plotting and MagIC 3.0 files and Pole naming convention
issue_body:
Here is an example of a contribution that is typical for a pole only contribution. It has two locations but four rows in the location table. The "Guyana Permo-Triassic Dykes" location is listed only once and should have its pole plotted by make_magic_plots.

The next three rows in the locations table are for the "La Quinta Formation Lavas". The first row, with all the sites averaged, and then the normal and reversed poles. I imagine we could use all of the three as plots, but the most important one is the first one that includes both reversed and normal.

Then it would also be nice to have a pole plot that has two points, the "Guyana Permo-Triassic Dykes" location and the main "La Quinta Formation Lavas".

So using the naming convention following the current one, we would want:
'LO:_' + 'Guyana Permo-Triassic Dykes' + '_POLE_map.' + fmt - The one pole given in the locations table 
'LO:_' + 'La Quinta Formation Lavas' + '_POLE_map.' + fmt - The combined pole. The second row in the table. Can be distinguished from the other ones with the same location name by the fact that the dir_polarity column is empty.

'MC:_' + '12012' + '_POLE_map.' + fmt - The two poles above plotted on the same plot. MC stands for magic contribution.

Then for the ones that have values in the dir_polarity column, One could create those and name them 
'LO:_' + 'La Quinta Formation Lavas' +'_n' +'_POLE_map.' + fmt
'LO:_' + 'La Quinta Formation Lavas' +'_r' +'_POLE_map.' + fmt

depending on the value in the dir_polarity column.

Magic Contribution file:
[magic_contribution_12012.txt](https://github.com/PmagPy/PmagPy/files/2199642/magic_contribution_12012.txt)

locations.txt file:
[locations.txt](https://github.com/PmagPy/PmagPy/files/2199641/locations.txt)

Currently on the pmag server, make_magic_plots uses polemap_magic.py to make this plot from this contribution:
![lo___ty__pole_map](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1278789/42788756-3d277ccc-8916-11e8-9ec2-4b92a52da94c.png)
Which looks empty and has an empty file name.

Según explica @fapetrone:

Creo que es el espacio de la URL que genera el siguiente reporte

```python
{u'error_code': 2,
      u'instance': u'http://www.energia.gob.ar/contenidos/archivos/Reorganizacion/informacion_del_mercado/publicaciones/energia_en_gral/balances_2016/balances/BEN 2015_final.xlsx',

      u'path': [u'dataset', 8, u'distribution', 0, u'downloadURL'],
      u'validator': u'format',
      u'validator_value': u'uri'},
```

Deberíamos:

1. Investigar brevemente si existe en la definición de URL/URI alguna restricción a la presencia de espacios en blanco en ellas.
2. En caso de existir dicha restricción,
  -  mantener el comportamiento actual del validador, y

Estimo la investigación sobre este tema en 2 horas. La duración del desarrollo de la funcionalidad dependerá del resultado de la investigación.

issue_title: Numpy interface corrupts array argument
issue_body:
There appears to be a platform-dependent bug in the numpy interface that results in corruption of the array passed to ``pyfftw.interfaces.numpy_fft.rfftn``. Under 32 bit Ubuntu 16.04 running on an Intel T2500 CPU, the following script

```
from __future__ import print_function

import hashlib
import numpy as np
import pyfftw

pyfftw.interfaces.cache.enable()

def rfftn(a, s=None, axes=None):
    return pyfftw.interfaces.numpy_fft.rfftn(a, s=s, axes=axes,
                overwrite_input=False, planner_effort='FFTW_MEASURE')

def hash(x):
    return hashlib.md5(x).hexdigest()[0:8]

np.random.seed(12345)
N = 64
U = np.random.randn(N, N).astype(np.float32)
V = np.random.randn(N, N).astype(np.float32)

for k in range(3):
    print(hash(U), hash(V))
    x = rfftn(U, None, axes=(0,1))
    y = rfftn(V, None, axes=(0,1))
```

gives output

```
a7662abd bb54ccca
a7662abd bb54ccca
a7662abd a7662abd
```

or

```
a7662abd bb54ccca
bb54ccca bb54ccca
bb54ccca bb54ccca
```

indicating that calling ``pyfftw.interfaces.numpy_fft.rfftn`` with ``U`` and then ``V`` results in ``U`` overwriting ``V`` or vice-versa.

This only seems to occur when the script is run with Python 3.5 (``python3`` on Ubuntu 16.04), and does not occur when the initial call to ``pyfftw.interfaces.cache.enable()`` is removed.

issue_title: Numpy interface can fail with non-writeable arrays.
issue_body:
The following code fails if it is called before a plan is cached:

```python
import numpy as np
from pyfftw.interfaces.numpy_fft import fat
y = np.zeros(4) + 1j  # Needs to be complex for failure
y.flags.writeable = False
fft(y)
```

```
In [5]: fft(y)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ValueError                                Traceback (most recent call last)
...
.../pyfftw/interfaces/_utils.pyc in _Xfftn(a, s, axes, overwrite_input, planner_effort, threads, auto_align_input, auto_contiguous, calling_func)
     81         a_copy = a.copy()
     82 
---> 83         FFTW_object = getattr(builders, calling_func)(*args)
     84 
     85         # Only copy if the input array is what was actually used
...
.../pyfftw/builders/_utils.pyc in _Xfftn(a, s, axes, overwrite_input, planner_effort, threads, auto_align_input, auto_contiguous, avoid_copy, inverse, real)
    171         if not avoid_copy:
    172             # Copy the data back into the (likely) destroyed array
--> 173             FFTW_object.get_input_array()[:] = a_copy
    174 
    175     return FFTW_object

ValueError: assignment destination is read-only
```

The problem is that the plan is made using the original array, which in this case is not writeable.  Since a copy is made, why not use it for planning instead since the copy will always be writeable?

The minimal change would be to set and restore the writeable flag:

```patch
82a83,85
>         # Input array must be writeable...
>         writeable, a.flags.writeable = a.flags.writeable, True
> 
89a93,95
>         # Restore writeable flag
>         a.flags.writeable = writeable
> 
```

Using the copy instead seems to work but there may be subtleties I am not aware of.

```patch
47,48c47,48
<         args = (a, s, axes, planner_effort, threads,
<                 auto_align_input, auto_contiguous)
---
>         args = [a, s, axes, planner_effort, threads,
>                 auto_align_input, auto_contiguous]
56,57c56,57
<         args = (a, s, axes, overwrite_input, planner_effort, threads,
<                 auto_align_input, auto_contiguous)
---
>         args = [a, s, axes, overwrite_input, planner_effort, threads,
>                 auto_align_input, auto_contiguous]
81c81
<         a_copy = a.copy()
---
>         args[0] = np.empty_like(a)
85,89d84
<         # Only copy if the input array is what was actually used
<         # (otherwise it shouldn't be overwritten)
<         if FFTW_object.get_input_array() is a:
<             a[:] = a_copy
< 
```

issue_title: Proposal: Ending Django 1.8, 1.9, 1.10 support
issue_body:
I'm proposing dropping Django versions older than 1.11 from a near-future Anymail update. (Django's 1.8 long-term-support period ended earlier this year, and Django 1.9 and 1.10 have been unsupported for a while.)

I would continue to release security fixes on the Anymail 3.x branch that supports Django 1.8, 1.9, and 1.10 through April, 2019. Also, if at all possible, Anymail 3.x would be patched if an ESP it supports makes a breaking change to their API.

But new Anymail features and new ESPs would only be available in Anymail 4.x with Django 1.11 or later.

If you're using Anymail with an older Django version and have any concerns about this, please comment.

class MemberListField(ListField):
